name: Deepen The Active PTX Hot-Band To A 3-Stage Pg2s Pipeline

summary.name: Deepen The Active PTX Hot-Band To A 3-Stage Pg2s Pipeline
summary.hypothesis: After restoring launch_bounds to 2, the PTX anchor regained low barrier and low short-scoreboard, but the remaining gap is now long_scoreboard plus mio_throttle at only 16.60% active warps. A 3-stage Pg2s ring should hide more global-to-shared latency by keeping one extra refill in flight, so the kernel can recover tensor activity without needing the failed register-interleave rewrite.
summary.expected_bottleneck: Global-to-shared latency hiding at low occupancy: long_scoreboard 5.45% and mio_throttle 3.98% indicate cp.async completion is still arriving too late for the current two-stage PTX pipeline.
summary.risk: Moderate-high. This touches the PTX prolog, the steady-state refill order, and the drain sequencing together, and it can easily grow shared memory or destabilize wait_group ordering if done sloppily.
ranking_notes: The current run already cleaned up barrier, so the remaining visible gap is the latency-hiding pair long_scoreboard plus mio_throttle.
This follows the Async Copy, Pg2s, and Stage guidance directly without replaying the exact register-interleave fingerprint that already failed.
It keeps the active PTX dispatch and only deepens overlap; if it loses, the result will still teach whether the current surface is pipeline-limited or occupancy-limited.
summary.stop_condition: Stop if the edit forces shared memory materially above the current PTX budget or if wait_group sequencing requires a broader launch-family rewrite.
expected_local_changes: Expand the PTX a_shared and b_shared ring from two stages to three.
Retune the PTX prolog and steady-state wait_group ordering to keep an extra refill in flight.
Leave the active PTX dispatch and grouped_rows constant unchanged in the first pipeline-depth probe.

notes: Node C may now implement this one candidate.
